author: jsign
content:
<@281967554572976128>, related to this, just worked on using the `eip-8025` feature in Ethrex for `ere-guests`. While doing that I found some problems in how `NewPayloadRequest` and `ExecutionPayload` definition against `consensus-specs` (thus `ere-guests` which was compliant).

Plus, I added a new entry point for hte guest program which receives the raw bytes, so we could push as much logic as possible that we had in `ere-guest` to rely on Ethrex logic as it should (e.g. new payload request hash tree root calc,  `NewPaylaodRequest` -> EL block conversion, validations, etc -- now basically `ere-guests` is a quite thin wrapper 😄 )

Ethrex PR whenever you have to take a look: https://github.com/lambdaclass/ethrex/pull/6516

If you're interested, here's the `ere-guests` PR that uses that branch PR which shows how ti simplified a lot of stuff: https://github.com/eth-act/ere-guests/pull/39 (No need to review -- just if you're curious).

Apart from moving Ethrex much closer to EIP-8025 specs and simplify ere-guests life setting a great north start for other ELs, I think this also helps <@699571793488510986> current experimentation, since these mismatches that I fixed could be confusing. He is working on moving `ExecutionWitness` to SSZ which would be the last remaining part to make the whoel raw bytes be SSZ, instead of current SSZ(newPaylaodRequest)+rkyv(ExecutionWitness) hybrid that Ethrex supports. (Although as I chatted with <@699571793488510986> (and I see <@427491045308235776> msg above), prob the best thing to do is to make `ExecutionWitness` be spec compliant also in structure rather than only SSZ encoded; but that can be the next step for Ethrex)
